free_text: -Measurement and pole-diagram analysis of the d(N,P)2N
cross-sections at a neutron energy of 14.5 MeV.
Time of flight, flight path of scattered
Ne-230, 50 millim. In diam. And 30 millim.
Thick used as target and detector
And 25 millim. Thick for neutron detection.
.Neutron and proton energy distribution at neutron
.The theoretical results obtained with a pole
diagram approximation agree rather well with the
measured ones except for e(proton) 4.2 to 5.5 MeV
and E(N') 3.0 to 5.0 MeV. Only low order approximation
.Data from priv. Comm., E. Arai, August 1971.
.Corrections for the dependence of the neutron
attenuation in the photomultiplier and its shielding
.No corrections made for neutron attenuation in the
Ti-t-target, in the target container, on the flight
path, in the target, since the neutron detector
was calibrated under the same conditions.
.The error of the secondary neutron energy is at 3 MeV
.The error of the proton energy is +- 4 percent.
